actors.name: APT28
actors.description: Russian GRU-linked group, one of the most prolific state-sponsored actors. Known for credential harvesting, spearphishing, and exploiting zero-days against Western governments and military targets.

actors.name: APT29
actors.description: Russian SVR-linked group responsible for the SolarWinds supply chain compromise and Microsoft corporate email breach. Highly patient, stealthy operators focused on long-term access.

actors.name: Sandworm
actors.description: Russian GRU Unit 74455. Responsible for the most destructive cyberattacks on record — Ukraine power grid attacks, NotPetya wiper, and Olympic Destroyer. Willing to cause collateral damage at scale.

actors.name: APT38
actors.description: North Korean group specialising in financial theft. Responsible for SWIFT attacks against central and commercial banks globally, stealing hundreds of millions. Increasingly targeting crypto exchanges.

actors.name: Lazarus Group
actors.description: North Korean umbrella group responsible for the largest cryptocurrency heists in history, including the $625M Ronin bridge attack. Also conducts espionage and destructive operations (Sony hack, WannaCry).

actors.name: Kimsuky
actors.description: North Korean group conducting intelligence collection targeting policy, defence, and financial institutions. Uses spearphishing, watering holes, and custom malware. Active against South Korean and Western targets.

actors.name: APT41
actors.description: Unique Chinese state-sponsored group that conducts both state-directed espionage and financially motivated cybercrime. Known for software supply chain compromises and wide-scale data theft.

actors.name: Salt Typhoon
actors.description: Chinese threat actor that compromised major US telecommunications providers in 2024, gaining access to lawful intercept infrastructure. One of the most significant intelligence collection operations against US comms infrastructure.

actors.name: Volt Typhoon
actors.description: Chinese group conducting stealthy, long-term pre-positioning inside US critical infrastructure — assessed as preparing for potential disruptive attacks in the event of a conflict over Taiwan. Uses living-off-the-land techniques.

actors.name: APT35
actors.description: Iranian IRGC-linked group conducting persistent spearphishing and social engineering campaigns against policy researchers, journalists, and dissidents. Increasingly targeting financial and critical infrastructure sectors.

actors.name: Handala
actors.description: Pro-Palestinian Iranian-linked hacktivist group deploying wiper malware against large enterprises. Claimed responsibility for wiping 200,000+ devices at Stryker (March 2026). Willing to deploy destructive capabilities at enterprise scale.
actors.recent_activity: Claimed responsibility for wiper malware attack on Stryker (medtech giant), reporting 200,000+ devices wiped. Escalation to destructive TTPs confirms group is capable of enterprise-scale damage.

actors.name: Scattered Spider
actors.description: English-speaking cybercriminal group known for sophisticated social engineering, SIM swapping, and helpdesk impersonation. Responsible for the MGM Resorts and Caesars attacks. Targets financial sector heavily via identity-based attacks.

actors.name: LockBit
actors.description: Prolific ransomware-as-a-service operation. Disrupted by Operation Cronos (Feb 2024) but resumed operations. One of the most financially damaging ransomware groups targeting the financial sector globally.

actors.name: BlackCat
actors.description: Sophisticated ransomware group using Rust-based malware. Executed the Change Healthcare attack (2024), causing massive disruption to US healthcare payments. Claimed exit scam and disbanded in early 2024, but affiliates remain active.

actors.name: Cl0p
actors.description: Russian-linked extortion group known for mass exploitation of file transfer vulnerabilities (MOVEit, GoAnywhere, Accellion). Prefers data theft and extortion over encryption. Responsible for breaches at hundreds of organisations simultaneously.

actors.name: Black Basta
actors.description: Ransomware group that emerged after the Conti shutdown, believed to include former Conti members. Targets financial sector and critical infrastructure. Internal chat logs leaked in 2025 exposed TTPs and affiliate network.

actors.name: Evil Corp
actors.description: Russian cybercriminal group responsible for Dridex banking malware and BitPaymer/WastedLocker ransomware. Sanctioned by US Treasury. Known for targeting banking credentials and executing fraudulent wire transfers.

actors.name: FIN7
actors.description: Long-running financially motivated group known for Carbanak malware used to steal over $1 billion from banks globally. Evolved into ransomware operations. One of the most prolific financial cybercrime groups of the past decade.

actors.name: RansomHub
actors.description: Rapidly growing ransomware-as-a-service operation that emerged in 2024 and quickly became one of the most prolific groups after absorbing affiliates from disrupted operations (LockBit, BlackCat). High attack volume across all sectors.

actors.name: BlackSanta
actors.description: Russian-speaking threat actor deploying a novel EDR-killing malware delivered via HR-targeted spearphishing. Active for 12+ months targeting HR staff as a soft entry point. A successful EDR kill blinds security teams entirely.
actors.recent_activity: New EDR killer malware strain identified. Delivered via spearphishing emails targeting HR departments. Tamper-protection policies and HR security awareness training should be reviewed immediately.
